questions.a: Infrastructure as a Service: virtualized compute, storage, and networking.
Platform as a Service: managed runtime and deployment environment.
Function as a Service: event-driven functions that run on demand.
A geographic cloud location containing multiple availability zones.
An isolated data center grouping inside a region.
A stateful network firewall attached to a cloud resource.
To keep internal resources off the public internet while still allowing outbound access through controlled paths.
They let clients access object storage without sharing permanent credentials.

questions.q: Why does least privilege matter in IAM?
questions.a: It limits blast radius if credentials or roles are compromised.

questions.q: Why is multi-AZ deployment common for production services?
questions.a: It survives the loss of one zone without full service outage.

questions.q: Why can egress cost surprise teams?
questions.a: Outbound data transfer is often billed and can dominate the bill.

questions.q: Why are serverless functions attractive for bursty workloads?
questions.a: They scale quickly and avoid paying for idle capacity.

questions.q: When should you choose a multi-region architecture?
questions.a: When you need geo-resilience or low latency for users spread across continents.

questions.q: When is a public subnet appropriate?
questions.a: When the resource must accept inbound internet traffic, such as a load balancer.

questions.q: What tradeoff comes with serverless cold starts?
questions.a: Lower idle cost, but higher latency on the first request after inactivity.

questions.q: Why might you still use VMs instead of managed services?
questions.a: You may need more control, custom software, or simpler portability.

questions.q: What tradeoff comes with heavily managed cloud services?
questions.a: You reduce ops burden but accept vendor lock-in and less low-level control.

questions.q: Why does IAM policy evaluation matter in troubleshooting?
questions.a: An action can fail because multiple policies, boundaries, or resource rules intersect.
